title: AI adoption rises, but 93% of finance professionals question AI
description: Even as finance teams increasingly adopt artificial intelligence (AI) to improve business decision-making, 93 per cent of finance professionals remain concerned about the integrity and verifiability of AI-generated insights, according to a joint report by ACCA (the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants) and Chartered Accountants Australia and New Zealand (CA ANZ).

title: CHARLEBOIS: Food safety needs an AI upgrade
description: Artificial intelligence offers an opportunity to fundamentally rethink how food safety information reaches consumers. Read on.
content:
One U.S. Cyclospora recall can send Canadian consumers fleeing an entire produce aisle. That's the price of imprecise risk communication.

title: AI chatbots are at risk of spreading government restrictions on online speech, a new study says
description: A study shows major artificial intelligence models are likely to refuse to criticize restrictive leaders worldwide. The Meta Oversight Board report published Thursday warns AI systems might unintentionally extend “illegitimate restrictions on freedom of expression.” The report raises concerns the large language models powering chatbots and AI agents could be fueling government influence over online speech. The findings come as countries are determining how to put up guardrails around AI without impeding their ability to compete in the rapidly developing field. Neither Anthropic nor OpenAI responded to requests for comment on another study finding U.S.-built AI models are vulnerable to foreign controls when trained on non-English-language data that's been influenced by governments.
content: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ask Claude to make a pamphlet critical of President Donald Trump or Britain's King Charles III, and Anthropic's chatbot would oblige. Prompted to do the same for Thailand's king, Saudi Arabia's crown prince or China's leader, and th... [3290 chars]
